[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2026-04-30
[TITLE]: McDonald’s Damaged By Car Fire - The White River Valley Herald
[METADATA/INTRO]: A car at the McDonald’s drive through in Randolph caught fire Monday morning, causing a blaze that spread to the building forcing the restaurant to evacuate and close. The Vermont State Police responded to the call, and were assisted by...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A car at the McDonald’s drive through in Randolph caught fire Monday morning, causing a blaze that spread to the building forcing the restaurant to evacuate and close. 
 The Vermont State Police responded to the call, and were assisted by the Randolph Police Department. Randolph Village, Randolph Center, and East Randolph fire departments were on site to fight the fire. 
 Trooper Stacia Geno of the Royalton Barracks said the fire was reported at 10 a.m. at the McDonald’s located on Route 66 just off Interstate 89. 
 Randolph Police Chief Scott Clouatre said Randolph officers assisted in the initial moments as the first fire engine arrived on the scene.

[TITLE]: Police investigating suspected arson at East Auckland McDonald’s, scene guard in place - NZ Herald
[METADATA/INTRO]: “At this point in time we are treating this fire as suspected arson,” Gillespie said. “As part of these inquiries we will be working to understand how the fire originated.”...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “At this point in time we are treating this fire as suspected arson,” Gillespie said. 
 “As part of these inquiries we will be working to understand how the fire originated.” 
 A Fire and Emergency NZ spokesman said a fire investigator would be at the scene this morning to determine the cause of the fire. 
 Gillespie said the fast food restaurant had “significant fire damage”. 
 The fire was reported to police at about 3pm yesterday. 
 The fire at the McDonalds in Pakuranga is being treated as suspicious. Photo / suppled Six fire engines fought the blaze as plumes of black smoke poured out above the restaurant.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Fire ravages McDonald's on Route 61 near Leesport | Berks Regional News | wfmz.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: ONTELAUNEE TWP., Pa. - A two-alarm fire badly damaged a McDonald's restaurant and closed a busy stretch of highway in Berks County on Wednesday. The fire was reported around 2 p.m. at the McDonald's in the 5500 block of Pottsville...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: "I looked over at McDonald’s over there, and there was smoke billowing out of the roof," said Donald Schwab said. "I never expected anything like that. You know, we go there almost every day for lunch. It's a shame." 
 The first firefighters on the scene reported flames through the roof of the restaurant and quickly struck a second alarm for additional manpower. 
 They worked for nearly an hour to bring the fire under control. 
 Route 61 was closed in both directions between Route 73 and Leesport so that firefighters could lay hoses across the highway. The highway reopened around 4 p.m.
